# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Pull Request Workflow

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, edited, reopened]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Run build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Notify build status
        env:
          PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
          PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
        run: |
          echo "Build triggered by PR: $PR_TITLE"
          echo "Description: $PR_BODY"

      - name: Commit changes
        run: |
          git config --local user.email "action@github.com"
          git config --local user.name "GitHub Action"
          git commit -m "Build triggered by PR: $PR_TITLE" || echo "No changes to commit"
